calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-01-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. COREY KNISS, Esq. of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (Tel: 213-380-2344) for petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local 1021 (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. WILLIAM MICHAEL QUINN, JR., Esq. (Tel: 415-241-6054) as Senior Deputy General Counsel for respondent San Francisco Unified School District.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021's Petition To Compel Arbitration.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner's motion to compel arbitration is denied.

The court decides the issue of arbitrability since petitioner does not cite a clear and unmistakable delegation clause. (See AT&T Technologies, Inc. v. Communication Workers of America, 475 U.S. 643, 649 (1986).) Here, petitioner waived/abandoned its right to compel arbitration.

Petitioner was aware of the Shum grievance by September 19, 2019. (Quinn Decl., Ex. 7.) Petitioner filed the grievance on December 19, 2019. (Petition, Ex. B.) The parties' collective bargaining agreement provides that petitioner has twenty-two (22) working days from the event giving rise to its complaint to file the grievance. (Petition, Exhibit A, at Art. 23.1.5 & Art. 23.2.2.2.) Here, the grievance was too late and abandoned. (See Id. [Art. 23.2.5 ("The Effect of Failure of Timely Action") (failure to submit grievance "within the required time limit at any step shall constitute an abandonment of the grievance")].).

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/SRB)
